AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN PLAINVIEW MINNESOTA AT 429 PM ON WEDNESDAY MAY 17TH. THE TORNADO PRODUCED SPOTTY MINOR DAMAGE ALONG A 0.6 MILE PATH. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 30 YARDS AND THE ESTIMATED PEAK WIND WAS 65 MPH.

A TORNADO DAMAGED TREES AND FARM BUILDINGS IN FAR SOUTHEAST TREMPEALEAU COUNTY WISCONSIN, MAINLY ALONG AND JUST NORTH OF HIGHWAY 54. THERE WAS WIND DAMAGE AROUND THE BRIEF TORNADO. THERE WAS ALSO WIND DAMAGE NOTED IN NEIGHBORING AREAS, INCLUDING LA CROSSE AND JACKSON COUNTIES. THE PATH LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 1.4 MILES WITH A MAX WIDTH OF 250 YARDS. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 95 MPH.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN ELGIN MINNESOTA ON THE EVENING OF MAY 17TH. THE TORNADO PRODUCED A SHORT PATH OF MAINLY TREE AND ROOF SHINGLE DAMAGE THROUGH THE CITY. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.2 MILES WITH A MAX WIDTH OF 30 YARDS. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 65 MPH.

UP TO 250 TREES WERE DAMAGED OR UPROOTED. THE TREE DAMAGE STARTED ON THE EAST END OF LAKE NESHONOC AND THEN CONTINUED TO THE NORTHEAST FOR 2 MILES. THE GREATEST LOSS OF TREES WERE NEAR LAKE NESHONOC. THREE FARMS WERE AFFECTED. IT DESTROYED 1 BARN. ANOTHER BARN LOST ITS SOUTH ROOF. THERE WAS ALSO MINOR DAMAGE TO A HOUSE AND A GARAGE. ONE OF THE FARMERS THOUGHT THAT THEY HAD LOST THEIR CHICKENS, BUT THEY WERE FOUND ROOSTED IN A PINE TREE AN HOUR LATER. THE STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 6

UP TO 250 TREES WERE DAMAGED OR UPROOTED. THE TREE DAMAGE STARTED ON THE EAST END OF LAKE NESHONOC AND THEN CONTINUED TO THE NORTHEAST FOR 2 MILES. THE GREATEST LOSS OF TREES WERE NEAR LAKE NESHONOC. THREE FARMS WERE AFFECTED. IT DESTROYED 1 BARN. ANOTHER BARN LOST ITS SOUTH ROOF. THERE WAS ALSO MINOR DAMAGE TO A HOUSE AND A GARAGE. ONE OF THE FARMERS THOUGHT THAT THEY HAD LOST THEIR CHICKENS, BUT THEY WERE FOUND ROOSTED IN A PINE TREE AN HOUR LATER. THE STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 60 TO 80 MPH.
